summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC web API, which underpins real-time communication like video calls, have focused on improving its reliability and functionality. One notable enhancement is the implementation of the `getRemoteCertificates` method, allowing applications to access the certificates being used in peer-to-peer connections, thus enhancing security by enabling better management of encryption credentials. Additionally, updates have eliminated unnecessary cache-clearing behavior and ensured that transport connections properly notify when they are closed, improving the accuracy of connection status updates. These changes collectively mean that developers will have a more robust framework for handling video and audio communications which, in turn, can lead to **more reliable and secure user experiences** in applications that rely on WebRTC.
